behavior_summary: When the player uses the 'U8P_BROKCHAR::look' (examine) action on this object/NPC, the script plays a single short bark reading "broken chair".
The U8P_BROKCHAR::look process then suspends/ends; there are no menu choices, branching conditions, global flag reads/writes, or external calls.
Overall behavior is purely descriptive and has no side effects on game state.
notable_control_flow: Single-entry process handler: U8P_BROKCHAR::look is invoked and immediately emits a bark, followed by a suspend/terminate of the process.
No conditional branches or calls are present; control flow is linear and short-lived.
open_questions: The raw representation included two 'Unknown' nodes (samples: '{', 'suspend') — do these represent in-engine bookkeeping or are they artifacts of disassembly?
Is the 'suspend' node intended to pause the U8P_BROKCHAR::look process until player input, or does it simply end the process immediately in this context?

main_function.name: U8P_BROKCHAR::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short descriptive response when the player examines this object/NPC; play a single bark "broken chair" and end the U8P_BROKCHAR::look process.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player performs the 'U8P_BROKCHAR::look' (examine) action on the object/NPC.
Classified as a U8P_BROKCHAR::look-type process (processType 0x0208) which the engine dispatches for examination events.

function_map.name: U8P_BROKCHAR::look
function_map.role: Examine/U8P_BROKCHAR::look handler that emits a single descriptive bark and terminates.
